messages.content: How many years does a President run in one term?
A President runs for one term of four years.
Who was elected President of the United States in 2016?
Donald J. Trump was elected President of the United States in 2016.
Which of these powers can only the government do? Declare war, Print money, Start an army, All of the above
All of the above are exclusively the government's powers to perform.
What is the capital of the United States?
The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.
What is the deadline to send in your taxes?

The deadline to send in your taxes is typically April 15th of each year. However, the deadline can vary slightly depending on weekends and holidays. Taxpayers can request an extension to file their taxes, which typically extends the deadline until October 15th.
Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
The Declaration of Independence was primarily drafted by Thomas Jefferson with input from other members of the Second Continental Congress, including John Adams and Benjamin Franklin.
When was the Declaration of Independence ratified?
The Declaration of Independence was ratified on July 4, 1776, by the Second Continental Congress.
Which of these was NOT one of the thirteen original states (colonies)? South Carolina, Delaware, New York, West Virginia
West Virginia was not one of the thirteen original states (colonies).
Which war divided our country?
The American Civil War, fought from 1861 to 1865, was a war that divided the United States.
In one word, what did Martin Luther King Jr. fight for?
Equality.
What federal holiday did President Biden proclaim in 2021?
President Biden proclaimed June 19, 2021, as Juneteenth National Independence Day, making it a federal holiday in the United States.
What is the longest river in the US?

The longest river in the United States is the Missouri River, which stretches for 2,341 miles (3,767 km) from its source in Montana to its confluence with the Mississippi River in Missouri. However, if the Missouri and Mississippi are considered a single river system, then the longest river in the US is the Mississippi River, which extends for 2,320 miles (3,734 km) from its source in Minnesota to its mouth in Louisiana.
Which of these is a Pacific Coast State? New York, Oregon, Nevada, Arizona
Oregon is a Pacific Coast State.
Which of these is an Atlantic Coast State? California, Michigan, Vermont, Georgia
Georgia is an Atlantic Coast State.
Which State does NOT border Canada? Wisconsin, Washington, Maine, Idaho
Idaho does not border Canada.
Wrong! It was Wisconsin.
You are correct. I apologize for the mistake. Wisconsin is the state that does not border Canada.
Which State is the Statue of Liberty located in? Washington, California, New York, Maine
The Statue of Liberty is located in New York.
What do the stripes on the flag represent?

The stripes on the flag represent the 13 original colonies that declared independence from Great Britain and founded the United States. There are 7 red stripes and 6 white stripes, alternating in color.
What do the fifty stars represent?
The fifty stars on the US flag represent the fifty states of the United States. Each star represents one state and together they symbolize the unity of the United States as a nation.
